title: Bridge Paper: Decoding haptic and imagined stimulus size in the human cortex.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal Reconstruction** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Mental imagery — visual imagery as real perception, imagination, pareidolia, aphantasia.
**Phase context:** mental imagery, visual imagery, imagination, aphantasia, mental simulation, pareidolia

Paper: Decoding haptic and imagined stimulus size in the human cortex.
DOI: 10.1016/j.neuroimage.2026.121774
Abstract: Human neuroimaging studies indicate that the early visual cortex (EVC), including the primary visual cortex (V1), is involved in haptic exploration of objects, even when visual information is not available. However, it remains unknown whether the features of haptically explored objects, like size, are represented in the EVC.
